title: Podman volume parent directory created are owned by root when using --user and --uidmap
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When mounting a configuration volume, the parent directory is owned by root, which prevent the container user from creating lateral configuration.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create user in image
`cid=$(buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora); buildah run $cid useradd -m user && buildah commit --rm $cid fedora-user`

2. Start the container
```
host$ podman run -it --rm --volume ~/.config/pavucontrol.ini:/home/user/.config/pavucontrol.ini --user 1000 --uidmap 1000:0:1 --uidmap 0:1:999 --uidmap 1001:1001:64535 fedora-user
[user@7246ce1da224 /]$ ls -la ~/.config
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 4096 Sep  9 20:14 .
drwx------. 3 user user 4096 Sep  9 20:14 ..
-rw-rw-r--. 1 user user    0 Sep  9 20:04 pavucontrol.ini
[user@7246ce1da224 /]$ mkdir ~/.config/pulseaudio
mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/home/user/.config/pulseaudio’: Permission denied
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Volume parent directory created by podman are not writable by the container user.


**Describe the results you expected:**

Volume parent directory should respect their parent owner uid or the volume target owner.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

A solution is to pre-create the directory structure:

```
host$ mkdir -p /tmp/pavucontrol-container/.config
host$ chcon -R system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0 /tmp/pavucontrol-container/
host$ podman run -it --rm --volume /tmp/pavucontrol-container:/home/user --volume ~/.config/pavucontrol.ini:/home/user/.config/pavucontrol.ini --user 1000 --uidmap 1000:0:1 --uidmap 0:1:999 --uidmap 1001:1001:64535 fedora-user
bash-5.0$ mkdir ~/.config/pulseaudio
bash-5.0$ ls -la ~/.config
total 12
drwxrwxr-x. 3 user user 4096 Sep  9 20:23 .
drwxrwxr-x. 3 user user 4096 Sep  9 20:21 ..
-rw-rw-r--. 1 user user    0 Sep  9 20:04 pavucontrol.ini
drwxr-xr-x. 2 user user 4096 Sep  9 20:23 pulseaudio
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.5.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.7
  podman version: 1.5.1
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.10.1
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.5.1-3.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.0, commit: d728afa06cd2df86a27f32a4692c7099a56acc97-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 464338944
  MemTotal: 16815693824
  OCIRuntime:
    package: containerd.io-1.2.6-3.3.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8
      commit: 425e105d5a03fabd737a126ad93d62a9eeede87f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: file
  hostname: ocp.rdocloud
  kernel: 5.2.9-200.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 121h 59m 13.33s (Approximately 5.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/fedora/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 9
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 37
  RunRoot: /tmp/1000
  VolumePath: /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.5.1-3.fc30.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The host is running in OpenStack

comments.body: This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@12factor-mx Interested in opening a PR?  @mheon has been off working on other issues/features...
Friendly ping. @mheon, do you have cycles to tackle this?

We don't actually make these directories - they're created by the OCI runtime when it goes to finalize the container's rootfs and make bind mounts.

@giuseppe Poke - do you think this is something crun/runc should do?

could it be a security issue if two containers running with different `--user` use the same volume?

This is something that could be easily fixed by creating the directory before running the container with the correct permissions.

The source directory should always be created by the user, if using a volume.  We could add a flag to the volume when we start running in random User Namespace, for the container engine to "Chown" the directory.

@ashley-cui Could you fix this bug.  Basically any directory created by podman within the container should match the parent directory.

In this case the podman is creating ~/.config/ as owned by root when it should have created it with the ownership of `~` "$HOME"

I don't think this is a bug.  I did the same thing on Docker and got the same results.

```
docker run -ti -v $PWD/Dockerfile:/test/Dockerfile --user bin fedora sh
sh-5.0$ id
uid=1(bin) gid=1(bin) groups=1(bin)
sh-5.0$ cd /test/
sh-5.0$ ls
Dockerfile
sh-5.0$ touch dan
touch: cannot touch 'dan': Permission denied
sh-5.0$ id
uid=1(bin) gid=1(bin) groups=1(bin)
```
